items.title: Verified `claude-code` docs require no monorepo updates in M C Ps ✅
items.summary: The PR confirms that the `claude-code` service referenced in Docs has no corresponding MCP server in this repository. The sync configuration and server inventory were reviewed, showing no mappings or directories for `claude-code`. No repository files were changed, ensuring the monorepo remains unaffected by the Docs update.

items.title: New SolRadar Research Report Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the latest SolRadar research report for the cycle dated 2026-05-07, now available under the reports directory. Key deliverables include comprehensive diagnostics, a watchlist review, and detailed candidate analyses, ensuring all necessary outputs are met. The report also addresses risk management by documenting partial degradation and maintaining source error visibility.

items.title: Add platform-tenant Caddy proxy alongside platform-frontend in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces a new Caddy deployment and service (`caddy-platform-tenant-proxy`) next to `platform-frontend` to handle sub-sites under platform.acedata.cloud. It includes a dedicated ConfigMap, PVC, Deployment, and LoadBalancer Service, ensuring isolated TLS state and separate traffic from Nexior's existing studio proxy. The deployment is applied via an update to `deploy/run.sh`, integrating with the existing CI/CD workflow without changing other services. This ensures correct routing and on-demand TLS for platform tenants without impacting studio tenants.

items.title: No changes applied as `claude-code` service is absent in M C Ps ⚡
items.summary: The sync process detected updates for `claude-code`, but this service is not present in the monorepo and is not mapped in `sync.yaml`. After auditing the repository, it was confirmed that no implementation changes were necessary. This no-op ensures that existing MCP services remain unaffected and avoids speculative edits.

items.title: Add comprehensive Nexior product docs for connectors, BYOK, Subsite, Studio, WebExtractor, and Claude Code 5-min in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds six new zh-CN documentation files covering key Nexior features: Connectors, BYOK, Subsite, Studio, WebExtractor, and a 5-minute Claude Code guide. All files are newly created, totaling 579 lines, with detailed setup instructions and usage scenarios for end users. These docs improve discoverability of platform capabilities and provide clear step-by-step guidance, reducing support queries. Translation automation will propagate content to 17 additional languages.

items.title: Add i18n key coverage guard to block incomplete locales in Frontend Web 🛡️
items.summary: Introduces a CI script that ensures every non-base locale matches the key set of zh-CN. The workflow now fails if translations are missing keys, preventing partial auto-translated PRs from merging. Updates include adding `check_i18n_coverage.py` and integrating it into both the CI and translation pipelines. This change improves reliability of the localization process without altering shipped code behavior.

items.title: Backfilled 930 missing i18n keys across 11 locales in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This PR restores 930 previously missing translation keys across 11 locales, including homepage, order, and pt site files. All affected JSON files were patched in place, preserving existing translations and placeholders. After the update, a full audit confirms zero missing keys, ensuring consistent multi-language rendering for AceDataCloud users.

items.title: Enable per-parent proxy CNAME for tenant domains in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This PR adds support for per-parent Site CNAME resolution for tenant custom domains. The helper `resolve_proxy_cname` now determines the correct Caddy reverse-proxy target based on the parent Site's metadata, defaulting to the global proxy if unset. `SiteDomainViewSet.create` snapshots this value on creation, ensuring DNS instructions show the correct proxy without affecting existing rows. Eight unit tests verify correct fallback and metadata handling.

items.title: Added deepseek-v4-flash and new Seedream generation options in M C Ps ⚡
items.summary: This update syncs MCP servers with the latest OpenAPI spec changes. The `aichat` service now includes `deepseek-v4-flash` in its model listings. `seedream_generate_image` gained `sequential_image_generation_options` for controlling batch image output and `optimize_prompt_options` for prompt optimization. All other services remain consistent with their current API specs.

items.title: Complete hands-on Nexior deployment guides for 7 platforms in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds detailed, step-by-step deployment sections to 7 Nexior documentation files, covering Docker, Vercel, Netlify, Sealos, Zeabur, GitCode, and Tencent Cloud. Each doc now includes build settings, environment variable references, post-deploy verification checklists, and platform-specific troubleshooting. No existing instructions were removed, ensuring current users retain the original tutorials. The changes total 528 insertions across all files, improving clarity and reducing common support issues.

items.title: Revamped top-funnel docs with current models and cURL examples in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This PR rewrites four long-form promotion articles in zh-CN, updating outdated content and adding real model integrations, working cURL examples, async callbacks, troubleshooting, and concrete scenarios. Chat API integration now covers 11 apps with detailed URL and API key guidance. Headshots, Luma, and Suno docs received expanded instructions, updated models, and removed deprecated examples or anti-pattern advice. Overall, the updates improve accuracy, usability, and developer onboarding for top-of-funnel content.

items.title: Automated Portuguese i18n updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This PR applies auto-generated translations from the zh-CN source to the Portuguese locale. Key updates include adding 134 new translations and removing 12 outdated entries in site-related JSON files. The changes enhance the internationalization coverage and improve user experience for Portuguese-speaking users.

items.title: Updated Model Matrix for OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and Nexior in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refreshes the model matrix across four key marketing documents, ensuring that all live models are accurately represented. Key additions include new tags for OpenAI's GPT-5.x series, Claude's latest Opus version, and several Gemini model updates. The changes enhance the clarity and completeness of our documentation, providing users with the most current information.

items.title: Clean up critical P0 docs issues and unsafe links in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This PR addresses five high-priority documentation issues in `docs/zh-CN/`. It removes an AI prompt leak from `terms.md`, deletes a misleading token reference in `support.md`, fixes stray characters in API URLs, eliminates hard-coded inviter IDs, and replaces a typosquatted Luma link with the official site. Changes improve accuracy, security, and clarity for end users. The translations for other languages will update automatically in the next CronJob run.

items.title: Revamped Documentation for Promotion APIs in Backend 🚀
items.summary: All 12 promotion_short_*.md documents in the Chinese documentation have been completely rewritten to include accurate model lists, cURL examples, and relevant use cases. This update resolves previous issues such as broken links, incorrect product information, and lack of technical content, enhancing clarity and usability for developers. The new format ensures each document provides concise value propositions and verifiable model details.

items.title: Simplify gpt-image-2 pricing to a flat rate in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update standardizes `gpt-image-2` costs to a single flat 0.09 cr per call, removing previous size-dependent tiers. All generation and edit requests now apply the same rate, simplifying both billing and service display. Legacy size-specific rules and whitelists were deleted, while other models remain unaffected. Verification tests confirm accurate calculation across multiple resolutions and edit cases.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translation updates based on changes from the zh-CN source. Key modifications include updates to various language files, enhancing the localization for multiple languages such as Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese. These changes aim to improve user experience by providing accurate translations across the platform.

items.title: Upgrade @types/node to v25.6.0 in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This update bumps the @types/node dependency from version 16.18.126 to 25.6.0 in the project. Both package.json and package-lock.json were updated, reflecting the new version and integrity hashes. The change ensures compatibility with the latest Node.js type definitions, improving type safety across the codebase.

items.title: Update @types/uuid to latest version in Frontend Web ⚡️
items.summary: This PR upgrades the @types/uuid package from version 8.3.4 to 11.0.0. The package-lock.json and package.json files were updated to reflect the new version. This ensures type definitions for UUIDs are current, improving type safety and compatibility with other dependencies. No functional changes were introduced.

items.title: Exciting New Site Management Features in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the wiring of four key tabs on the site detail page, enhancing the user experience for managing general information, domains, subsites, and advanced settings. A new `siteDomain` operator has been added, along with a build-flag-gated navigation entry for the Sites console, ensuring a smoother integration with existing features. Localization improvements have also been made to support multiple languages.

items.title: Polish order detail page with labeled payment tags in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: Updated the order detail page to render Payment Method as colored tags instead of plain text, improving readability and visual consistency. Removed bold styling from the Amount field so it aligns with other description rows. Added helper methods for tag labeling and type selection, simplifying the template logic. ESLint and TypeScript checks passed with zero errors.

items.title: Fix aspect ratio detection for nano-banana edits in Platform Service 🎨
items.summary: This update restores the functionality to snap detected aspect ratios for the nano-banana image editing feature. Previously, the aspect ratio was being discarded, causing all outputs to default to a square format. The fix ensures that the correct aspect ratio is passed to upstream services, preserving the original image proportions.

items.title: Automatic i18n Updates from zh-CN in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automatic translation updates for internationalization (i18n) from the zh-CN source. Key files such as order.json for English, French, and Portuguese have been modified to reflect these changes, enhancing the multilingual support of the platform. Several cache files were also removed or renamed as part of the cleanup process.

items.title: Display Reward payment and credit amounts clearly in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update adds support for displaying 'Reward' as a payment method and surfaces the associated credit amount on the order detail page. Previously, reward orders showed an empty payment method and lacked credit information. Now, reward orders display both the method and credits, while paid top-ups also show credit counts alongside USD prices. The changes include Vue template updates and i18n additions for English and Chinese.

items.title: Enhance Reward Order Descriptions with Credit Count in Backend 🎉
items.summary: This update modifies the order description for survey rewards to include the credit count, making it more user-friendly. Previously, the description displayed an internal alias, which was not meaningful to users. Now, it will read as 'Survey reward — X credits', improving clarity for users viewing their orders.

items.title: Increase onboarding survey max selections to 6 in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The onboarding survey's `use_cases.max_select` has been raised from 3 to 6 to better accommodate users with multiple needs. Corresponding hints in both English and Simplified Chinese have been updated. A new migration refreshes the schema and translation rows without affecting other locales or options, ensuring existing deployments serve the correct limits.

items.title: Streamline CDN Path by Renaming Prefix in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `openaihk` prefix from the CDN path for images, aligning it with existing ephone outputs. The new path format will be `/gpt-image/<task_id>_<idx>.<ext>`, ensuring consistency across services without altering response shapes. The change affects only the CDN path constant in the codebase, with no impact on existing URLs.

items.title: Show full HTTP request and response logs in X402 Client 🔍
items.summary: This update enhances `test-skale-e2e.ts` by adding verbose logging for all HTTP calls, capturing complete URLs, headers, and truncated request/response bodies. Both unauthenticated probes and paid retries now print detailed timing and gateway headers for better visibility during stage testing. No production code or protocol changes were made, ensuring the gateway behavior remains identical. Documentation and sample outputs were updated to reflect the new logging format.

items.title: Switch to fast GPT chat demo + on-chain settlement display in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This PR updates the SKALE demo to use GPT chat completions instead of Suno, reducing end-to-end run time to under a second. It prints detailed on-chain settlement information after successful API calls and pretty-prints the signed EIP-712 envelope. Bug fixes include correcting the default SKALE RPC endpoint and updating the FAQ on gas payments. Changes improve demo clarity and reliability while ensuring accurate payment verification.

items.title: Completed comprehensive SolRadar research cycle in Sol Radar 📊
items.summary: Executed a full research cycle and published the resulting report, fully populating the seeded template with provider diagnostics, candidate analysis, and machine-readable intents. Reviewed 29 candidates, skipping 13 for low liquidity, and updated hold/skip decisions with detailed reasoning and metrics like top10_holder_pct and creator_rug_count. Data source health was documented, including Grok provider timeouts and errors, ensuring full traceability of results.

items.title: Auto-translate i18n updates in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated internationalization (i18n) translations from the zh-CN source. New language files have been added for Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese, enhancing accessibility for a broader audience. Additionally, the English site.json has been modified to improve clarity and consistency.

items.title: Launch dedicated OpenAIHK /images deployments for cortex routing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: Introduces a dedicated `platform-service-openaihk-openai` deployment mirroring the public `/images/{generations,edits}` endpoints. This isolates vendor-specific quirks from the shared worker, handling URL-to-binary conversion, COS rehosting, and `channel: cortex` labeling. The new handlers support both JSON and multipart inputs, keeping response shapes compatible with existing clients. Kubernetes manifests and deployment scripts are updated to deploy and expose this new service.

items.title: Scaffold Affiliate Console for Site Management in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces a feature-flagged scaffold for the affiliate and sub-site management console, including site operators, routes, and skeleton pages. Key additions include the SiteOperator for API interactions, new routes for site management, and initial page structures for site details and listings. The console is gated by a build-time flag to ensure it does not affect the production bundle until fully rolled out.

items.title: Enhance Site Metadata for Pricing Control in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a validated `metadata.pricing` block for each Site, allowing affiliates to adjust the platform price without affecting distribution. Key features include a `markup_ratio`, currency normalization, and a controlled `applies_to` field. The implementation is supported by extensive unit tests ensuring robust functionality.

items.title: Enhance Image Upload Experience with New Cropper in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update replaces the generic upload button in the site-settings panel with a new image cropper for logos, favicons, and QR codes. The cropper, ported from AuthFrontend, ensures users can only upload images that meet the recommended dimensions, improving the overall user experience. Additionally, new internationalization keys have been added for better guidance during image uploads.

items.title: Fix site state persistence issue in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where the site state persisted in localStorage could lead to incorrect branding being displayed when visiting the login page without a `?site=` parameter. The fix ensures that when no site is found, the persisted site state is cleared, allowing the default AceDataCloud branding to be shown. This change enhances the user experience by preventing unintended branding from appearing.

items.title: Fix custom domain resolution for bound Sites in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses an issue where custom domains bound to existing Sites were incorrectly resolving to new, empty Sites. The root cause was identified in the lookup process, which only considered the original Site identifier. The fix implements a new method to ensure that requests for bound custom domains return the correct Site, preventing unauthorized admin access and improving user experience.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI Image Routing with Cortex Channel in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new routing option for the OpenAI image generation and editing endpoints, allowing users to specify `channel: cortex` for accessing the `gpt-image-2` model. The implementation ensures that requests with the cortex channel are directed to the appropriate provider without affecting existing functionalities. Additionally, symmetry is maintained by tagging the xiaojing channel for the reverse model.

items.title: Add English onboarding survey seed to fix UI fallback in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This PR adds a new migration that seeds English translations for the onboarding-2026 survey. Previously, English users saw Chinese text because the initial migration only included zh-cn keys and the CronJob delay caused a fallback. Now, 110 English keys are created hand-written, ensuring immediate correct display for new users. Other locales remain unaffected and continue to be handled by the regular CronJob.

items.title: Normalize Gemini-3 fallback keys to prevent 500 errors in Platform Service ⚙️
items.summary: This update fixes a latent fallback bug for Gemini-3 models where `get_account()` failed to find valid substitutes, causing 500 errors. The fallback keys in `model_fallbacks.json` are now normalized to match upstream model names, ensuring proper lookup. Existing price checks remain unchanged, and non-Gemini fallback chains are unaffected. After the fix, requests automatically route to the next available model when all primary providers are down.

items.title: Expanded multi-language survey support in Frontend Web 🌐
items.summary: This PR adds auto-translated survey content from zh-CN to multiple languages including Arabic, German, Spanish, French, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Russian, and Traditional Chinese. The English survey strings were also refined for clarity and reward messaging. These updates ensure users across regions see consistent and accurate survey prompts and feedback notifications.

items.title: Introducing a Schema-Driven Survey Dialog in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update implements a fully schema-driven SurveyDialog component in the console, allowing for dynamic survey rendering based on backend templates. It includes features for user interaction, such as reward notifications upon survey completion, and integrates localization for multiple languages. The changes enhance user engagement and streamline survey management for admins.

items.title: Launch generic survey engine with atomic rewards in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The survey system was migrated from AuthBackend to a generalized engine on PlatformBackend, enabling onboarding, NPS, and churn surveys to be defined as data rather than code. SurveyTemplate and SurveyResponse models were added with JSON-based schemas, triggers, and reward rules. Submissions now run atomically, creating Orders and crediting users without risk of duplicate rewards. All API endpoints, admin views, and seed data for onboarding surveys are included and verified.

items.title: Refine model selection for gpt-image-2 requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the model selection logic to ensure that the high-res variant swap only occurs for explicit `gpt-image-2` requests. It introduces an explicit `requested_model` argument in the `resolve_upstream_model()` function, improving clarity and preventing future issues with implicit assumptions. The changes affect three files, streamlining the routing process for image generation and editing.

items.title: Tightened mobile chat spacing to prevent awkward text wraps in Nexior 📱
items.summary: Updated the mobile chat layout to reclaim horizontal space on narrow screens by reducing author gutter width, avatar sizing, and bubble padding below the 640px breakpoint. The change adds roughly 24px of usable text width on small devices, improving paragraph wrapping and eliminating single-word last lines in assistant messages. The update is limited to scoped SCSS in Message.vue with no template or JavaScript changes.

items.title: Clean up mobile resize listeners to prevent leaks in Nexior 🔧
items.summary: Refactored Main.vue and Console.vue to use named resize handlers and remove them on component unmount. This prevents accumulation of anonymous window resize listeners on repeated navigation, which could retain unmounted component instances and degrade performance on mobile. No reactive behavior changes were made; mobile breakpoint logic remains unchanged. The updates ensure smoother and more memory-efficient mobile navigation.

items.title: Enable pinch-zoom on web and Android while preserving iOS workaround in Nexior 🔍
items.summary: This update modifies the viewport meta at runtime to allow pinch-zoom on web and Android, while keeping the iOS Capacitor input-focus zoom workaround intact. The change ensures accessibility compliance with WCAG 1.4.4 and allows users to zoom into images or screenshots. Native shells remain unaffected, maintaining existing UX on iOS.

items.title: Optimize console list pages for 360px phones in Nexior 📱
items.summary: This update adjusts the console list pages—order, usage, and application—to display properly on small mobile screens. Key changes include reducing the scroll-panel padding in Console.vue and hiding secondary el-table columns below 640px. Desktop layouts remain unchanged, ensuring mobile users can access essential columns without horizontal scrolling. The modification impacts 5 files with a net addition of 53 lines and removal of 10 lines.

items.title: Clamp dialogs on small screens to prevent overflow in Nexior 📱
items.summary: Added a global CSS rule to clamp `.el-dialog` widths on screens narrower than 540px, ensuring payment, wallet, confirmation, and chat dialogs no longer overflow. This approach preserves deliberately small dialogs while keeping the close button reachable and vertical scrolling functional. No JavaScript changes were required, and all affected dialogs were visually verified on multiple iPhone presets. The update maintains consistency with Element Plus mobile breakpoints and existing gutters.

items.title: Fix iOS safe-area layout for navigators, drawer buttons, and wallet pill in Nexior 📱
items.summary: This update ensures all mobile layouts in Nexior respect iOS safe-area insets. The wallet pill, bottom navigator, and drawer toggle buttons now adjust using `env(safe-area-inset-*)` and the viewport meta is updated with `viewport-fit=cover`. Changes apply across 23 files, mainly CSS adjustments, without impacting Android or web layouts. Visual tests on iPhone simulators confirm proper spacing above the notch and home indicator.

items.title: Route 2K/4K image requests to the high-res variant in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This PR updates image routing for the xiaojingai pool so that 2K and 4K size requests use the `gpt-image-2-reverse-4k` model instead of being downgraded to 1K. Changes include a new `resolve_upstream_model` function and integration in both `images_generations.py` and `images_edits.py`. Verification shows full-resolution output now matches requested dimensions, with 4K images taking ~150–180s upstream. All other models and 1K requests remain unchanged.

items.title: Auto-translate i18n updates in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces auto-generated i18n translation updates from the zh-CN source, enhancing the localization of the application. New translations have been added for multiple languages, including Arabic, German, Spanish, and more, while existing English translations have been refined. These changes aim to improve user experience by providing a more accessible interface for a diverse audience.

items.title: Fix tenant branding on auth pages in Frontend Web ✨
items.summary: This update ensures that custom tenant logos, titles, and favicons correctly display on authentication pages. The `getSiteOrigin` function now returns a bare lowercase hostname matching backend storage, resolving previous mismatches. The site initialization also updates the document title and favicon dynamically. AuthFrontend now fully supports Nexior's tenant-aware branding without backend changes.

items.title: Upgrade ESLint Standard config to 17.1.0 in Frontend Web ⚡️
items.summary: This PR updates the `eslint-config-standard` dependency from version 16.0.3 to 17.1.0. The update adds Node engine support, compatibility with the latest `eslint-plugin-n`, and new `maxBOF` settings for `no-multiple-empty-lines`. It also updates related package lock entries to reflect the new version, ensuring consistent linting rules across the codebase.

items.title: Add GPT-image-2 deployment and service for ephone in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: Introduces Kubernetes Deployment and Service for the `platform-service-ephone-images` worker, enabling `/images/generations` and `/images/edits` endpoints to serve traffic. Updates `deploy/run.sh` to apply the new resources. Uses a separate replica pool to isolate long-running image generation jobs from other ephone subservices, maintaining predictable latency for existing endpoints.

items.title: Add SKALE x402 live demo walkthrough in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: Introduces `docs/SKALE_DEMO.md`, a full end-to-end guide for running a live x402 micropayment demo on SKALE. The walkthrough covers API calls resulting in `402 Payment Required`, signing EIP-3009 transactions, and successful on-chain settlement on SKALE Europa. All steps use real data from the 2026-05-07 production run, making it fully reproducible for presentations. No code changes were made; this is a documentation-only addition.

items.title: Mirror OpenAI image payloads to CDN and backfill prompts in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This PR updates the OpenAI image generation paths to replace large `b64_json` payloads with CDN URLs, reducing payload size for clients. It also ensures `revised_prompt` is populated when missing, maintaining compatibility with Nexior task cards. Tencent COS integration was added for async uploads, and configuration values were wired in the production deployment. Existing image pools and opt-out paths remain unchanged.

items.title: Fix(welcome): only toast on actual first global application creation in Nexior 🐛
New SolRadar Research Cycle Published in Sol Radar 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the scheduled SolRadar research cycle for May 7, 2026, as a comprehensive report. Key enhancements include detailed metrics on candidates reviewed, explicit intents, and degradation handling notes. The report aims to improve decision-making processes with clear actions and reasons for each candidate.
